differential_diagnoses: Type A lactic acidosis due to distributive/septic shock
Hypercapnic encephalopathy due to respiratory failure and non-adherence to BiPAP
Secondary adrenal insufficiency due to chronic inhaled corticosteroid use
next_steps: Obtain arterial blood gas with serum lactate, electrolytes, and anion gap calculation
Perform morning cortisol and ACTH stimulation test to rule out adrenal insufficiency
Maintain hemodynamics with vasopressors and aggressive source control for potential urosepsis
Continue BiPAP support and monitor serial CO2 levels
Consider renal replacement therapy if lactic acidosis is severe and refractory to conservative management
